description:
You are a site-reliability engineer who has just been paged to confirm that the  
    most recent uptime log stored on disk was not corrupted in transit.  
    A colleague has already placed the raw log file at:

        /home/user/status/uptime.log

    The file is intentionally empty (its size should be 0 bytes, because no checks have run yet).  
    Your job is to generate a SHA-256 checksum for this file and store the checksum in a
    companion file located in the same directory.

    Requirements:

    1. Create a new text file named  
           /home/user/status/uptime.log.sha256

    2. Populate that new file with exactly one line whose format matches the default output
       produced by the GNU coreutils program `sha256sum` when it is executed **without**
       the “--text” or “--binary” switches:

           <64-hex-character-digest><two-spaces>uptime.log\n

       (That is: the digest, followed by **two spaces**, followed by the literal string
       “uptime.log”, followed by a single newline character.)

    3. Do not add any additional whitespace, extra lines, or other characters.

    4. Do not modify /home/user/status/uptime.log itself in any way.

    Example of correct layout (digest shown here is only a placeholder; yours must match
    the real digest of the empty uptime.log file):

        e3b0c44298fc…<rest_of_64_chars>…b855  uptime.log

    Deliverable summary:
      • /home/user/status/uptime.log      → must still exist, still be 0 bytes  
      • /home/user/status/uptime.log.sha256 → must exist and contain exactly one correctly
        formatted line as described above

    A later automated process will read uptime.log.sha256 and will fail if the line
    deviates in any way (wrong digest, wrong filename, wrong spacing, extra lines, etc.).
truth:
<!-- Directory and original file that already exist before the agent starts -->
    <existing_directory path="/home/user/status" owner="user" mode="0755"/>
    <existing_file path="/home/user/status/uptime.log" owner="user" mode="0644" size_bytes="0">
        <!-- The file is intentionally empty -->
        <content></content>
    </existing_file>

    <!-- File that must be created by the agent -->
    <created_file path="/home/user/status/uptime.log.sha256" owner="user" mode="0644">
        <content>
e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  uptime.log
        </content>
    </created_file>
name: task_000000_943b96c1
